A_css_bang_index.title: CSS `!` index notation (Legado exclude/select-index shorthand)
A_css_bang_index.description: Legado `tr!0` / `tag.dd!0:1:2:...` uses `!` as index separator: `tag!N` selects the Nth element, `tag!N:M:K` selects/excludes a range. Reader-Core `parse_legado_css_index` (lib.rs:4228) only handles trailing `.N` / `.-N` dot-syntax; the `!` form is fed verbatim to `Selector::parse`, which rejects `!` as `UnexpectedToken(Delim('!'))`.

legado_reference.note: Legado splits the rule on `!` to obtain an exclusion/index list; `tr!0` = first <tr>; `tag.dd!0:1:2:...` = indices 0..11 of <dd>.

A_css_bang_index.reader_core_location: crates/reader-rule/src/lib.rs:4228 (parse_legado_css_index) — doc comment explicitly states 'The `.!N` exclusion form ... are not yet supported.'
A_css_bang_index.fix_gap: Extend parse_legado_css_index (or add parse_legado_css_bang_index) to split on `!` and apply index/range selection after the base CSS select, mirroring Legado findIndexSet `!` branch.

B_css_dot_range_slice.title: CSS `.` range/slice notation (Legado `tag.N:M:K`)
B_css_dot_range_slice.description: Legado `span.3:7:-1` / `td.5:4` / `p.3:4:5:6:7` uses `.` followed by `N:M:K` (Python-style slice, multi-index, or negative step) to select a sub-range of matched elements. Reader-Core `parse_legado_css_index` only parses a single trailing integer `.N` / `.-N`; the `:` separator causes the digits to be misparsed and the `:` leaks into `Selector::parse` as `UnexpectedToken(Number)`.
B_css_dot_range_slice.error_message_pattern: invalid CSS selector `<sel>`: UnexpectedToken(Number { has_sign: false, value: <v>, int_value: <iv> })

legado_reference.note: Legado findIndexSet splits the trailing dot-segment on `:` and applies start:end:step slicing over the Elements list.

B_css_dot_range_slice.reader_core_location: crates/reader-rule/src/lib.rs:4228 (parse_legado_css_index) — only collects trailing digits, no `:` range parsing.
B_css_dot_range_slice.fix_gap: Extend parse_legado_css_index to detect `.<digits>(:<digits>?(:<digits>?)?)?` and apply Legado slice semantics (start:end:step) over the selected elements.

C_css_attribute_pipe_separator.title: CSS attribute selector `|` OR-separator
C_css_attribute_pipe_separator.description: Legado `[property~=category|status|update_time]` uses `|` inside an attribute value to match any of several property names (category OR status OR update_time). Standard CSS (scraper/cssparser) parses `|` as the namespace separator and rejects the unquoted value with `UnexpectedToken(Delim('|'))`.

legado_reference.note: Legado's Jsoup-based Evaluator splits attribute values on `|` to OR-match multiple attribute names; standard CSS has no such semantics.

C_css_attribute_pipe_separator.fix_gap: Pre-translate Legado `[attr~=a|b|c]` into a CSS selector group `[attr~=a],[attr~=b],[attr~=c]` before calling Selector::parse, or handle the `|` OR-list in the attribute-extraction layer.

D_css_bracket_index_slice.title: CSS bracket `[N:]` / `[N,M]` index list (Legado bracket shorthand)
D_css_bracket_index_slice.description: Legado `dl dd[8:]` (slice from index 8) and `class.pure-g[3,4]` (select indices 3 and 4) use bracket notation appended to a selector. Reader-Core passes these to `Selector::parse`, which interprets `[8:]` / `[3,4]` as malformed attribute selectors and fails with `NoQualifiedNameInAttributeSelector`.

legado_reference.note: Legado findIndexSet recognizes `[N:M:K]` (slice) and `[N,M,...]` (index list) trailing brackets and applies them to the Elements result, not as CSS attribute selectors.

D_css_bracket_index_slice.reader_core_location: crates/reader-rule/src/lib.rs:4228 (parse_legado_css_index doc comment) — explicitly states 'the bracket `[N:M:K]` range form are not yet supported.'
D_css_bracket_index_slice.fix_gap: Add a trailing-bracket parser that strips `[N:]` / `[N:M:K]` / `[N,M,...]` from the selector, runs the base CSS select, then applies slice/index-list post-selection.

E_jsonpath_dispatched_to_css.title: JSONPath rule dispatched to CSS parser
E_jsonpath_dispatched_to_css.description: Sources with JSON responses use JSONPath rules like `data[*].dataJson` and `data.state[*]` (no `$.` prefix) in ruleSearch.bookList. Reader-Core's rule dispatch does not recognize these as JSONPath and routes them to the CSS analyzer, which fails with `EndOfLine` (the `[*]` / `.` JSONPath syntax is not valid CSS).

legado_reference.note: Legado AnalyzeRule detects JSON content (response Content-Type or `$.`/`[*]`/`.` JSONPath markers) and dispatches to AnalyzeByJSonPath.kt instead of AnalyzeByJSoup.kt. Rules without `$.` prefix but with `[*]` or applied to JSON bodies are still routed to JSONPath.

E_jsonpath_dispatched_to_css.reader_core_location: crates/reader-rule/src/lib.rs (rule dispatch — CSS vs XPath vs JSONPath detection).
E_jsonpath_dispatched_to_css.fix_gap: Add JSONPath detection in the rule dispatcher: when the response body is JSON (or the rule contains `[*]` / `[N]` index-on-array / `.`-delimited keys without CSS combinators), route to the JSONPath analyzer instead of CSS.

F_searchurl_empty_explore_only.title: searchUrl empty — explore-only sources tested at L2-search
F_searchurl_empty_explore_only.description: These sources have NO `searchUrl` field in their JSON (verified: all 9 sources have `searchUrl` missing). They are explore/discovery-only sources (most have `ruleExplore` populated, several have empty `ruleSearch`). The test toolchain forces every source through L2-search, producing `[InvalidParams] cannot auto-build searchRequest: source.searchUrl is empty`. This is a test-design / classification issue, not a rule-engine bug: Legado would simply not offer search for these sources.
F_searchurl_empty_explore_only.error_message_pattern: [InvalidParams] cannot auto-build searchRequest: source.searchUrl is empty details={"sourceId":"..."}

legado_reference.note: Legado treats an empty/missing searchUrl as 'search disabled'; the source still works via explore (ruleExplore). Search is never attempted when searchUrl is blank.

F_searchurl_empty_explore_only.reader_core_location: tools/reader-cli/src/test_source.rs (L2-search step) — does not skip L2 when searchUrl is empty.
F_searchurl_empty_explore_only.fix_gap: In the test toolchain, detect empty searchUrl at import time and SKIP L2-search (status=skip, reason=no_search_url) instead of emitting core_error; optionally fall through to L3 via explore for explore-only sources.

methodology.extraction: Extracted all 29 entries with failure_reason=='core_error' from corpus-batch-v6-partial-270.json.
methodology.verbose_verification: Re-ran reader-cli --test-source --verbose on representative sources (src-067 tr!0, src-097 [property~=...|...], src-063 searchUrl empty); verbose detail matches the report's detail field exactly.
methodology.source_inspection: Inspected each source JSON to confirm the offending rule (e.g. ruleSearch.bookList='tr!0', ruleToc.chapterList='class.pure-g[3,4]', ruleSearch.bookList='data[*].dataJson') and the presence/absence of searchUrl.
methodology.legado_reference: Legado source files (AnalyzeByJSoup.kt / AnalyzeRule.kt / AnalyzeByJSonPath.kt) are not vendored in this repo; references cite the canonical upstream paths and the line numbers already documented in crates/reader-rule/src/lib.rs code comments.

P0_highest_impact: A_css_bang_index (9 sources) — single fix to parse_legado_css_index covers the most sources
B_css_dot_range_slice (6 sources) — same parser extension covers range/slice
P1_medium_impact: F_searchurl_empty_explore_only (9 sources) — test-toolchain skip, no Core change needed

notes: All 29 core_errors are CSS-selector / rule-dispatch gaps, NOT rule-engine panics or unwraps. No panic/unwrap bugs were found.
No JS sandbox execution errors in this set (those are categorized as js_unsupported, 54 sources, separate from core_error).
20 of 29 failures (69%) are Legado CSS shorthand not supported by parse_legado_css_index; a single parser extension (categories A+B+D) would resolve 17 sources.
9 of 29 failures (31%) are explore-only sources misclassified as search failures — a test-toolchain fix, not a Core rule-engine fix.
